text: [warm, intimate] There you are. Before we begin — look up at the house in front of you. The Wilder House, built in eighteen ninety-eight, back when Savannah was dressing her old bones in new Victorian finery. Heart-pine and wrought iron, and a hundred-some years of Savannah evenings soaked into the walls. [settling] A man named Wilder lived here once. A reverend. He's long gone now — but in this city, gone doesn't mean gone. Savannah remembers everything, and everyone. And tonight, she remembers him for you. [handing off] He knows these streets better than any map. So I'm going to let the Reverend himself walk you the first stretch — from his own front steps, up through the old blocks, to the edge of Forsyth Park. Listen close. Here he is.
